name: Climb to Collado de la Palmerica (El Chive) — from Los Gallardos
name_original: Subida al Collado de la Palmerica (El Chive) — desde Los Gallardos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Los Gallardos traverse a diverse landscape, situated in a wide valley between the Sierra Cabrera and Sierra de Bedar mountain ranges. The terrain offers varied cycling experiences, from routes along the Rambla de las Chozas to climbs within the surrounding hills. This region provides a mix of challenging ascents and scenic loops through rural countryside and citrus groves. Best road cycling routes around Los Gallardos The most popular road cycling route is Climb to Collado de la Palmerica (El Chive) — from Los Gallardos, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the local mountains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Granatilla Viewpoint – In the middle of the desert loop from Turre, a difficult 51.0 miles (82.1 km) path. This demanding loop offers expansive views across desert landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Villaricos Archaeological Site – Cala Cristal Views loop from Los Tres Amigos, a 70.6 miles (113.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and historical sites, often completed in about 5 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Los Gallardos is defined by mountain ranges like Sierra Cabrera and Sierra de Bedar, ramblas, and coastal views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Los Gallardos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 10,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Gallardos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Los Gallardos?
A: Los Gallardos offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 100 tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy scenic rides to challenging climbs through the mountains.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes around Los Gallardos?
A: The routes around Los Gallardos offer a diverse range of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 19 easy routes, 34 moderate routes, and 44 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels, from beginners to experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes in Los Gallardos?
A: Road cycling routes in Los Gallardos traverse a diverse landscape. You can expect to ride through wide valleys, alongside the Rambla de las Chozas, and into the surrounding Sierra Cabrera and Sierra de Bedar mountain ranges. The scenery often includes rural countryside, citrus groves, and expansive views, sometimes even stretching towards coastal areas or desert landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Los Gallardos is well-suited for experienced riders seeking a challenge. Many routes feature significant elevation gains through the local mountains. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987973089/climb-to-collado-de-la-palmerica-el-chive-from-los-gallardos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Collado de la Palmerica (El Chive) — from Los Gallardos&lt;/a&gt; covers over 52 km with substantial climbing, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31941605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granatilla Viewpoint – In the middle of the desert loop from Turre&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 82 km loop with over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or attractions can I visit on a road cycling tour?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by interesting attractions. You might encounter miradors with lovely views over the local landscape towards the hills of Bedar. For historical and cultural sights, consider routes that lead towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful white Moorish village, or explore areas with remains of Roman houses and ancient Arab fortifications. Some routes also pass by castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3821572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Macenas Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3581863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Escobetas Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes around Los Gallardos. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaricos Archaeological Site – Cala Cristal Views loop from Los Tres Amigos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a long coastal and historical ride.

Q: Is Los Gallardos a good base for exploring other areas by road bike?
A: Absolutely. Los Gallardos benefits from good road connections, including the A-92 motorway and direct access off the AP-7, making it an excellent base. From here, you can easily access diverse areas like the Cabo de Gata Natural Park, which is only about half an hour away, offering further opportunities for varied cycling experiences.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Los Gallardos?
A: The road cycling routes around Los Gallardos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from mountain climbs to scenic loops through rural countryside and coastal views, as well as the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there options for bike rental in Los Gallardos?
A: Yes, bike rental services are available in the area. For instance, Camping Los Gallardos offers bike rentals and local experts who can provide advice on routes tailored to your fitness level and desired ride length.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Los Gallardos?
A: While specific seasonal details vary, the region's climate generally makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Winter can also be mild, providing a good escape from colder climates elsewhere.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near villages and towns where you can find cafes and pubs. Los Gallardos itself, and nearby settlements like Mojácar, offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments and a break. It's always a good idea to check your chosen route for specific points of interest or towns along the way.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: Los Gallardos and surrounding towns typically offer parking options. Depending on your chosen starting point, you can often find public parking areas in villages or designated spots near popular trailheads. It's advisable to check specific parking availability for your intended route's starting location.
tips: It is a small town located at the eastern end of the Sierra de los Filabres. Half of the town is located on a pair of rocky outcrops that elevate the houses. The church dominates the rest.
Ascent coming from the center of El Chive. There is approximately two and a half kilometers of uphill to reach the Collado de la Palmerica and then descend towards Lubrín.
After the Ascent of the Campito, there is a descent towards the town of Bédar and where you can see the set of views of the Sierra Lisbona until you reach the Mediterranean Sea itself.

waypoints.name: Descent to Bédar with views of Sierra Lisbona and the Mediterranean Sea
